recipe_data.title: Strawberry Summer Cake
recipe_data.description: A reliable muffin-tin build for Strawberry Summer Cake, designed for crispy edges and a tender center.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Grease a standard 12-cup muffin tin well.
Whisk eggs, milk, melted butter, salt, and pepper until fully combined.
Whisk in flour just until smooth, then fold in cheddar and green onion.
Divide batter evenly among muffin cups, filling each about 3/4 full.
Bake 20-24 minutes, until puffed, lightly golden, and just set in the center.
Rest 5 minutes in pan, then loosen with a thin knife and serve warm.
recipe_data.chef_notes: Use a metal pan for better browning. Let the cups rest before removing so they hold shape.
margaret_notes: At least we're doing the technique correctly.
Check oven temp with a thermometer. Don't trust the dial.
Use a metal pan for better browning. Let the cups rest before removing so they hold shape.

dialogue.message: Fine. FINE. Genoise, macerated strawberries, lemon syrup, stabilized whipped cream. No edible flowers or charcoal. Lock by 5pm; I'll nail the recipe.
If I may offer some context, the lemon syrup should rest 12 hours to bloom and the strawberries be macerated with a whisper of balsamic—trust you, of course. M
Margaret, that direction is perfect. Marcus, can you add the 12-hour syrup rest and whisper of balsamic to the doc? I think maybe we could lock by 5pm?
Fine. 12-hour lemon syrup rest. Strawberries: whisper balsamic only. No flowers or charcoal. I’ll proof the doc and lock it by 5. *mutters under breath*
If I may offer some context, I’ll draft the line—"Lemon syrup rested 12 hours; strawberries macerated with a whisper of balsamic"—and drop it into the doc. M
Thanks Margaret, Marcus—this is great. Sorry, but could we possibly add one line on stabilizing whipped cream? I think maybe we could lock at 5pm?
Stabilize whipped cream: 1 tsp gelatin per cup—bloom 5 mins, melt, cool; whip to soft peaks and chill. Fine. I’ll lock doc by 5. *mutters under breath*
If I may offer context, I'd phrase it: 'Stabilize whipped cream: bloom 1 tsp gelatin per cup 5 minutes; melt, cool; whip to soft peaks and chill.' M
Sorry, but can we use Marcus's phrasing and lock by 5pm? I think maybe we could possibly add "see notes for photos." Does that make sense?
Fine. Use Marcus's phrasing and add "see notes for photos." I'll proof, tidy gelatin wording if needed, and lock the doc by 5. *mutters under breath*
Test trays cooling. I’ll lock ratios and send the draft by 4:30. No edible flowers. Measure twice. *mutters under breath*
Thanks, Margaret — that sounds perfect. Sorry, quick Q: keep lemon-zest variant or fully nix? I think maybe we could possibly keep a tiny option. Does that make sense?
If I may offer some context: keep a tiny lemon‑zest option for whom brightness is essential — it livens without grandstanding. Measure twice, Margaret. —M
Fine. Keep a tiny lemon‑zest option — 3g zest per dozen, separate batter, same bake temp. No edible flowers. Measure twice. *mutters under breath*
Thanks Margaret and Marcus — perfect. Sorry, small ask: can someone note "3g zest per dozen, separate batter" in the doc? I'm probably wrong, but feels important. Thanks!
I'm thinking about the visual language — lighting tests dropped. Negative space is important. We need one decisive hero; styling: linen, vintage fork, subtle eucalyptus. Final by 5pm.
Sorry, I think maybe we could possibly warm the lighting slightly and use parsley instead of eucalyptus—feels more homey. Final by 5pm still good? Does that make sense?
Warm the lighting slightly. Parsley, yes — eucalyptus makes me clench my jaw. One clear hero, linen and vintage fork okay. Decide, Steph. *mutters under breath*
Okay, warm lighting, parsley not eucalyptus. One decisive hero, linen and vintage fork. I'm thinking about the visual language here. Final by 5pm.

copy_text.body: There is a certain poetry in the simple act of cooking. Strawberry Summer Cake represents more than mere sustenance—it is a meditation on the intersection of tradition and innovation, of comfort and creativity. The muffin tin, that most humble of vessels, transforms ingredients into something greater than the sum of their parts.
copy_text.literary_references: MFK Fisher once observed about eggs...

dialogue.message: If I may offer context: let's keep evocative tone but tighten copy. Proposed headline: 'Strawberry Summer Cake — Sun, Slice, Memory.' Choose by 1pm; I’ll submit 2:30. M
I think maybe we could possibly go with "Strawberry Summer Cake — Sun, Slice, Memory." Marcus, that works for me if no one objects by 1pm?
Fine. FINE. I like it but trim the florals—'Strawberry Summer Cake — Slice Into Sunlight.' Clear, evocative, not saccharine. *mutters under breath*

positive: I love this! The concept is really strong.
review_data.notes: Let's circle back if anyone has concerns?

dialogue.message: 5pm final. No eucalyptus garnish. Steph—decide filling now. I'll re-test crumb and glaze, measurements twice. That's not how ratios work, but what do I know. *mutters under breath*
Sorry, but could we possibly go lemon curd for the filling — bright, holds up to bake, easy ratios. I'm probably wrong, but seems safest?
I'm thinking about the visual language here... lemon curd is smart — bright and bake-stable. Can we finish with a glossy torched meringue ribbon and candied lemon slice?
If I may offer some context... lemon curd is wise; torched meringue ribbon is dramatic but watch moisture; candied lemon adds nostalgia — I'll bring French press coffee. M

Staging mostly green; hit an image CDN invalidation snag. Rolling a hotfix now — expect staging clear by 11:45. Yeah I can probably automate that.

Okay team — 5:00pm publish is firm for Strawberry Summer Cake. Please confirm image and copy now so I can hit publish. I'm probably wrong, but I need it.
Fine. FINE. Image okay but glaze looks flat in full-res — increase gloss shot. Copy's fine; change "fresh" to "just-picked." You have my approval after that.
